preamble: The historical loss of the palatal nasal /ɲ/ to a zero sound is a documented phonetic transformation, particularly observed in the development of certain Romance languages.

examples.note: The Latin sequence /nj/ typically palatalized to /ɲ/ in early Romance, which subsequently deleted in Romanian.

sources: Zampaulo, A. (2019). Palatal sound change in the Romance languages: diachronic and synchronic perspectives. Oxford University Press.
